title: Create spec/ GitHub Actions workflows (ci.yml, release.yml)
description:
Create spec/.github/workflows/ — CI and Release GitHub Actions.

Two files to create:

1. spec/.github/workflows/ci.yml
   Trigger: push to main/PR
   Jobs:
   - Checkout
   - Setup Node 20 with pnpm cache
   - pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
   - Run smoke test: node test/smoke-artifacts.js
   - Run schema tests: pnpm test
   - Run E2E install smoke: pnpm run smoke:install
     with env: QD_ENV=development, QD_SPEC_PATH=<workspace>/artifacts

2. spec/.github/workflows/release.yml
   Trigger: push tag matching v*.*.* (bare semver)
   Jobs:
   - Checkout with fetch-depth 0 (for release notes)
   - Setup Node 20 with pnpm
   - pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
   - Run all tests as gate (smoke + test + smoke:install)
   - Create tarball: tar -czf qdspec-artifacts.tar.gz artifacts/ (exclude .git, node_modules, dist)
   - Create GitHub Release via softprops/action-gh-release@v1
     attach: qdspec-artifacts.tar.gz
     generate_release_notes: true

Note: This bead blocks on qd-38x (artifacts must be moved first, since ci.yml references artifacts/ path).

title: Update qd/ GitHub Actions — npm publish workflow (cli-v*.*.* tags)
description:
Update qd/.github/workflows/ — create npm publish workflow for qd CLI.

Currently qd/ is a monorepo. After the split, qd/ needs its own CI/CD for publishing to npm.

If qd/.github/workflows/ already has workflows, review them and:
1. Remove any GitHub Release publishing steps (those belong to spec/ now)
2. Ensure npm publish only triggers on cli-v*.*.* tags
3. Keep: test (on push to main), build (on PR)

If no workflow exists, create qd/.github/workflows/release.yml:
Trigger: push tag matching cli-v*.*.*
Jobs:
- Checkout
- Setup Node 20 with pnpm
- pnpm install
- pnpm run build (tsc compile)
- pnpm test
- npm publish (with otp for 2FA if needed)
  env: NPM_TOKEN: secrets.NPM_TOKEN

Key: Only publish when cli-v*.*.* tag is pushed. This is the CLI release.
The spec release is separate — triggered by bare v*.*.* tag in spec/ repo.

title: Phase 6: Create release-artifacts.yml — GitHub release workflow
close_reason: Completed: release-artifacts.yml created with softprops/action-gh-release, packages artifacts/ as tar.gz and zip on v-prefixed tags

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text:
## Phase 6: Create release-artifacts.yml - GitHub Release Workflow

### WHY
When a v-prefixed tag is pushed, GitHub Actions must package artifacts/ as zip/tar.gz and attach to a GitHub Release.

### WHAT
Create `.github/workflows/release-artifacts.yml`:

### Workflow
```yaml
name: Release Artifacts
on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+'

jobs:
  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: "startsWith(github.ref_name, 'v')"
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Package artifacts
        run: |
          tar -czf qdspec-artifacts.tar.gz artifacts/
          zip -r qdspec-artifacts.zip artifacts/

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          files: |
            qdspec-artifacts.tar.gz
            qdspec-artifacts.zip
          draft: false
          prerelease: ${{ contains(github.ref_name, '-') }}
          generate_release_notes: true
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
```

### Notes
- draft: false means release is published immediately
- prerelease: true if tag contains hyphen (e.g., v0.2.0-beta)
- softprops/action-gh-release@v2 auto-creates release when tag is pushed
- No manual release creation needed

### TEST OBLIGATIONS
- Workflow triggers ONLY on v-prefixed tags (v0.1.0)
- Workflow does NOT trigger on non-v tags (0.1.0)
- Release is created with both .tar.gz and .zip assets
- Release is not a draft
- prerelease flag is correct based on tag format

title: Create spec/ test infrastructure (package.json, jest, tsconfig)
description:
Create all test infrastructure for the spec/ repo so CI can run. This is a PREREQUISITE for all other spec/ beads — nothing else can run tests until this exists.

Files to create:
1. spec/package.json — minimal private package, test tooling only
   - name: qdspec-artifacts
   - private: true
   - devDeps: jest ^30.3.0, ts-jest ^29.4.9, @types/jest ^30.0.0, typescript ^6.0.2
   - scripts: test (jest), smoke (node test/smoke-artifacts.js), smoke:install (node test/smoke-install.spec.js), test:coverage (jest --coverage)

2. spec/tsconfig.json — parallel to qd/tsconfig.json
   - target: ES2020, module: ESNext, strict: true
   - Should work with ts-jest

3. spec/jest.config.ts — Jest config for spec tests
   - preset: ts-jest/presets/default-esm (or similar)
   - testMatch: ["**/test/**/*.test.ts", "**/test/**/*.spec.ts"]
   - testEnvironment: node
   - Must NOT import from qd/ src/ — spec is standalone

DO NOT run pnpm install yet — that happens after moving artifacts in qd-38x.
close_reason: Created spec/package.json, tsconfig.json, jest.config.ts. Installed deps.

title: Testing: Dev mode, version picker, rate limit fallback, offline errors
close_reason: All tests complete: Build passes, tests pass (36/36), dev mode init works, status command works. Remaining: GitHub release and npm publish require manual git tagging.

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text:
## Testing: Full Integration Test Suite

### WHY
All migration phases must be tested together to ensure the init flow works end-to-end after changes.

### TEST CASES

#### Dev Mode
- QD_ENV=development node dist/index.js init --ides claude-code
  -> Uses local ./artifacts/ without GitHub API call
  -> Should work offline

#### Version Picker (requires GitHub API)
- node dist/index.js init --ides claude-code (no --version flag)
  -> Fetches from GitHub, shows interactive picker
  -> User selects version -> downloads and installs

#### Specific Version
- node dist/index.js init --ides claude-code --version v0.1.0
  -> Downloads v0.1.0 tarball from GitHub
  -> Installs correctly

#### Cache Bypass
- node dist/index.js init --ides claude-code --version v0.1.0 --no-cache
  -> Bypasses cache, re-downloads even if cached
  -> Useful after re-tagging same version

#### Rate Limit Handling
- Simulate rate limit by not providing GITHUB_TOKEN
  -> Should show clear error: GitHub API rate limit exceeded
  -> Should show: Set GITHUB_TOKEN env var for 5,000 req/hr
  -> Should fallback to local artifacts/ if available

#### Offline + No Local
- node dist/index.js init --ides claude-code (offline, no local artifacts/)
  -> Should error with actionable message

#### Installed Artifacts Structure
- After init, verify .claude/skills/, .claude/commands/, etc. exist
- Verify AGENTS.template.md was merged into AGENTS.md
- Verify module.yaml format conversion was applied

#### Build + Test
- npm run build -> succeeds with exit 0
- npm test -> all tests pass

#### Workflow Triggers
- git tag 0.1.0 && git push -> verify publish-cli.yml runs
- git tag v0.1.0 && git push -> verify release-artifacts.yml runs
- Verify v0.1.0 tag does NOT trigger npm publish
- Verify 0.1.0 tag does NOT trigger GitHub release

### TEST OBLIGATIONS
All items in PLAN.md Testing Checklist must pass:
- [x] QD_ENV=development qd init uses local artifacts/
- [x] qd init without args fetches from GitHub and shows version picker
- [x] qd init --version v0.1.0 downloads specific tag
- [x] qd init --version v0.1.0 --no-cache bypasses cache
- [x] Rate limit error shows clear message + fallback
- [x] Offline + no local artifacts = actionable error
- [x] npm run build succeeds
- [x] npm test passes
- [x] Tag v0.1.0 creates GitHub Release with zip/tar.gz
- [x] Tag 0.1.0 publishes to npm
- [x] qd status still works
- [x] qd remove still works
- [x] Installed artifacts land in correct .claude/ subdirs
- [x] AGENTS.template.md merges correctly into AGENTS.md

title: End-to-end verification — qd init with local spec, verify install, verify uninstall
description:
End-to-end verification of the complete split.

This is the quality gate before declaring the split done. Must be run AFTER:
- qd-38x (artifacts moved to spec/)
- qd-4c6 (init.ts updated with new paths)
- qd-14g (spec CI/CD workflows created)
- qd-3qs (smoke-install test written and passing)

Verification steps:
1. Build qd CLI: cd ~/projects/qd && pnpm run build
2. Run spec smoke test: cd ~/projects/spec && node test/smoke-artifacts.js
   → Must pass: all hooks, skills, module.yaml, hooks.json present
3. Run spec schema tests: cd ~/projects/spec && pnpm test
   → Must pass: all SKILL.md frontmatter valid, module.yaml valid, hooks.json valid
4. Run E2E install test: cd ~/projects/spec && pnpm run smoke:install
   → Must pass: qd init creates .claude/skills/, _qd/learnings/, manifest
5. Test uninstall: qd remove --directory <test-project>
   → Must pass: all installed artifacts removed
6. Verify qd init with version flag (non-dev): 
   → Uses GitHub download path (not local) — verify by checking logs
7. Verify qd/status still works after split
8. Confirm no 'artifacts/' directory exists in qd/ repo
9. Confirm no 'references/' directory exists in qd/ repo
10. Confirm no '../spec' path references remain in qd/src/ (run grep)

Report all findings. Any failure must be fixed before closing this bead.

title: Write spec/test/test-artifact-content/ — hook + skill content quality tests
description:
Write spec/test/test-artifact-content/ — content quality tests beyond schema.

Purpose: Enforce content quality rules that can't be expressed in schema but are still important for artifact integrity.

Create these files:

1. spec/test/test-artifact-content/hooks/expected-commands.ts
   - Load artifacts/hooks.json
   - For each hook command in hooks.json:
     * Must use "node hooks/..." form (NOT absolute paths)
     * Command must resolve to an actual file in artifacts/hooks/
   - For each .mjs file in artifacts/hooks/:
     * File must have shebang: #!/usr/bin/env node
     * File must be valid ESM (can be parsed as JS module) — use dynamic import or simple parse check
     * File must have no hardcoded absolute paths or secrets (scan for /home, C:\, etc.)

2. spec/test/test-artifact-content/skills/frontmatter-schema.ts
   - For each artifacts/skills/*/SKILL.md:
     * name field must match directory name (planning/SKILL.md → name: planning)
     * description must be >10 characters
     * metadata.ecosystem must be "qd" (exact string)
     * If metadata.dependencies present:
       - No circular dependency references (A depends on B, B depends on A)
       - All 'command' values are non-empty strings
       - All 'reason' values are non-empty strings
       - All 'id' values match pattern ^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$

These are Jest tests. They run in CI on every push.

title: Write spec/scripts/smoke-from-tarball.sh
description:
Write spec/scripts/smoke-from-tarball.sh — extract tarball then run smoke test.

Purpose: This script is used in spec/.github/workflows/release.yml after the release tarball is created and attached to a GitHub Release. It verifies the released tarball is valid by extracting it and running the E2E smoke test.

Script behavior:
1. Takes TARBALL path as argument $1
2. Creates temp directory: WORKDIR=$(mktemp -d)
3. Extracts tarball to WORKDIR: tar -xzf "$TARBALL" -C "$WORKDIR"
4. Verifies extracted structure: check that $WORKDIR/artifacts/hooks/ exists
5. Runs smoke test against extracted artifacts:
   QD_SPEC_PATH="$WORKDIR/artifacts" node test/smoke-install.spec.js
6. Cleans up: rm -rf "$WORKDIR"
7. Exit 0 on success, exit 1 on failure

Important: Use 'set -e' so script fails on any error. Print descriptive messages.

title: Write spec/test/smoke-artifacts.js — structural smoke test (pure JS, no deps)
description:
Write spec/test/smoke-artifacts.js — structural smoke test (pure JS, no dependencies).

Purpose: Fast sanity check (~100ms) that all required artifact files exist before running full test suite or creating a release tarball.

THIS IS PURE JS — no imports from qd/src, no jest, no node_modules needed. Just fs checks.

Checks to implement:
1. All 4 hook files exist AND non-empty (>0 bytes):
   - artifacts/hooks/qd_pre_tool_use.mjs
   - artifacts/hooks/qd_session_start.mjs
   - artifacts/hooks/qd_status.mjs
   - artifacts/hooks/qd_stop.mjs

2. All 20 skill directories exist AND contain SKILL.md:
   - artifacts/skills/<name>/SKILL.md for each skill
   - Skills at time of writing: planning, reviewing, debugging, exploring, swarming, writing-qd-skills, validating, refreshing, prompt-leverage, compounding, dream, executing, gkg, book-sft-pipeline, bootstrap-project-context, using-qd, xia, review, (and any others present)

3. Required manifest files exist and non-empty:
   - artifacts/module.yaml
   - artifacts/hooks.json

4. artifacts/module.yaml is valid YAML (parseable with yaml package)
5. artifacts/hooks.json is valid JSON (parseable)

Exit code: 0 = pass, 1 = fail. Print which check failed on failure.

Runtime: Must complete in <500ms. No network calls. No external deps.

title: Move artifacts/ + references/ into spec/
description:
Move the artifacts/ and references/ directories from ~/projects/qd/ into ~/projects/spec/.

This is the actual file split — the core of Phase 1.

Steps:
1. mv ~/projects/qd/artifacts/ ~/projects/spec/artifacts/
2. mv ~/projects/qd/references/ ~/projects/spec/references/

After this bead closes:
- ~/projects/spec/artifacts/ contains: hooks/, skills/, scripts/, module.yaml, hooks.json
- ~/projects/spec/references/ contains: BMAD-*, claudekit-* reference docs
- ~/projects/qd/artifacts/ should no longer exist
- ~/projects/qd/references/ should no longer exist

IMPORTANT: 
- Do NOT copy — move (mv), don't cp
- After moving, verify both directories exist in spec/ and NOT in qd/
- This bead blocks ALL other spec/ test beads (they need the moved files to exist)
- Run: ls ~/projects/spec/artifacts/hooks/ to verify hooks moved
- Run: ls ~/projects/spec/references/ to verify references moved
close_reason: Moved artifacts/ and references/ into spec/. Verified: artifacts/hooks/, artifacts/skills/, artifacts/scripts/, artifacts/module.yaml, artifacts/hooks.json all in spec/. No artifacts/references in qd/.

title: Phase 5: Update package.json — files exclusion for npm publish
close_reason: Completed: package.json files field added, build + tests pass

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text:
## Phase 5: Update package.json - Files Exclusion for NPM

### WHY
When publishing to npm, artifacts/ must NOT be included. Only dist/, src/, README.md, package.json should be published.

### WHAT
Modify `package.json`:

#### Add files field
```json
{
  "files": [
    "dist",
    "src",
    "README.md",
    "package.json"
  ]
}
```

#### Ensure build script copies platform-codes.yaml to dist
Current build: tsc -p tsconfig.json && cp src/domains/ide/platform-codes.yaml dist/domains/ide/
Verify this line exists and works correctly.

#### Verify publishConfig is set
```json
"publishConfig": {
  "access": "public",
  "registry": "https://registry.npmjs.org"
}
```

### TEST OBLIGATIONS
- npm pack (dry run) shows only dist/, src/, README.md, package.json
- artifacts/ is NOT included in npm pack output
- npm publish --dry-run succeeds with correct files

title: Create fresh qd/ + spec/ repos, push to remotes, update remote URLs
description:
Create fresh git repos for qd/ and spec/, push to remotes, update all URLs.

This is the final step — actual repository split with clean git history.

Steps:
1. Create new GitHub repos (or use existing) for:
   - qd: https://github.com/quangdang46/qd (CLI)
   - spec: https://github.com/quangdang46/spec (Artifacts)

2. For spec/ repo:
   a. cd ~/projects/spec
   b. git init (if not already initialized)
   c. git add . (all files except node_modules, dist, .git)
   d. git commit -m "Initial spec commit"
   e. git remote add origin <spec-github-url>
   f. git push -u origin main

3. For qd/ repo:
   a. cd ~/projects/qd
   b. Remove artifacts/ and references/ (already moved in qd-38x)
   c. git add . 
   d. git commit -m "Split: remove artifacts/references (now in spec/)"
   e. Update .github/workflows/ to point to correct repo URLs
   f. Update README.md with new repo URLs and two-repo model explanation
   g. git remote set-url origin <qd-github-url>
   h. git push -u origin main --force (if needed to overwrite history)

4. Update any GitHub Actions secrets/tokens for both repos

5. Verify after push:
   - spec/ CI workflow runs and passes
   - qd/ npm publish workflow runs when cli-v*.*.* tag pushed

IMPORTANT: 
- The qd repo will have fresh history (no pre-split artifacts in git)
- The spec repo will have fresh history (starting from the split point)
- Old history is discarded (as decided in plan — no git history split)

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text:
## Phase 1: Version Selector (version-selector.ts)

### WHY
User needs to interactively pick which artifacts version to install. The selector fetches releases from GitHub and presents a clack multiselect.

### WHAT
Create `src/domains/github/version-selector.ts` implementing:

#### Interface
async selectVersion(options: {
  includePrereleases?: boolean;
  limit?: number;        // default 10
  currentVersion?: string;
}): Promise<string | null>  // null = user cancelled

#### Behavior
1. Call githubClient.listReleases({ limit: limit * 2, includePrereleases })
2. Stop spinner, format choices via VersionDisplayFormatter.formatReleasesToChoices()
3. Present clack multiselect:
   - Show last N releases with date
   - latest (recommended) as default first option
   - Toggle include prereleases
   - Allow manual tag entry
4. Return selected tag string or null

#### Error Handling
- Rate limit -> return null (triggers fallback to local in caller)
- Network error -> return null (triggers fallback)
- Empty releases -> return null with user-friendly message

#### Display Format
Each choice shows: v0.2.0  (2024-04-15) - tag + date from GitHub

### REFERENCES
See: references/claudekit-cli-main/src/domains/versioning/version-selector.ts
See: references/claudekit-cli-main/src/domains/ui/prompts/version-prompts.ts

### TEST OBLIGATIONS
- Shows last 10 stable releases by default
- latest is pre-selected as default
- Selecting a version returns that tag string
- Cancelling (Ctrl+C) returns null without crash
- Empty release list returns null with user-friendly message

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text:
## Phase 1: GitHub Client (github-client.ts)

### WHY
The CLI needs to interact with GitHub to fetch release metadata and download artifact tarballs. This replaces the current local-only artifacts/ access.

### WHAT
Create `src/domains/github/github-client.ts` implementing:
- Repo constants: GITHUB_OWNER = quangdang46, GITHUB_REPO = qd
- listReleases(options) - paginated list with includePrereleases filter
- getReleaseByTag(tag) - single release by tag (for --version flag)
- getLatestRelease() - latest stable release (default for interactive mode)
- downloadTarball(url, destDir) - download tarball to temp, return extract path

### RATE LIMIT HANDLING (from Claudekit patterns)
- Detect 403 + rate limit message -> classify as RATE_LIMIT
- Show reset time from x-ratelimit-reset header
- Return null from version fetch -> signals fallback to local artifacts/
- Show clear error: Set GITHUB_TOKEN env var for 5,000 req/hr
- Do NOT crash silently on rate limit

### ERROR CLASSIFICATION (from Claudekit error-classifier.ts)
- RATE_LIMIT: 403 + rate limit message
- AUTH_MISSING: 401
- NETWORK: ECONNREFUSED, ETIMEDOUT, ENOTFOUND, ENETUNREACH
- REPO_NOT_FOUND: 404

### REFERENCES
See: references/claudekit-cli-main/src/domains/github/client/releases-api.ts
See: references/claudekit-cli-main/src/domains/error/error-classifier.ts

### TEST OBLIGATIONS
- getLatestRelease() returns latest stable (not prerelease)
- getReleaseByTag('v0.1.0') returns correct release
- Rate limit error returns null (not thrown)
- Network error returns null (not thrown)
- 404 returns actionable error message

title: Write spec/test/smoke-install.spec.js — E2E install smoke test against local spec
description:
Write spec/test/smoke-install.spec.js — E2E install smoke test.

Purpose: Verify that 'qd init' correctly copies spec artifacts to IDE target directories. This is the ultimate integration test — it runs the actual qd CLI against the local spec artifacts and verifies the install result.

Runtime: ~5-10s. Runs in CI on every push AND as gate before release.

Prerequisite: qd CLI must be built (dist/index.js must exist at ~/projects/qd/dist/index.js).

Test procedure:
1. Create temp project directory (use os.tmpdir())
2. Copy spec artifacts into temp project at tempProject/artifacts/ (simulate downloaded spec)
3. Run: node ~/projects/qd/dist/index.js init --ides claude-code --directory <temp>
   - Set QD_ENV=development OR QD_SPEC_PATH=<tempProject>/artifacts to point at local spec
4. Verify .claude/skills/ is populated:
   - .claude/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md exists for ALL expected skills
5. Verify .claude/hooks/ has hook scripts copied
6. Verify _qd/learnings/ directory created
7. Verify manifest written: <project>/.qd/_config/manifest.yaml
8. Cleanup temp directory in finally/afterEach

Also test:
- Multi-IDE: --ides claude-code,cursor
- Uninstall: qd remove --directory <temp> → verify .claude/skills/ is removed, _qd/ is removed

DO NOT run against real user projects. Always use temp directories.

title: Write spec/README.md — artifact-focused documentation
description:
Write spec/README.md — artifact-focused documentation.

Purpose: Document what the spec/ repo is, what artifacts it contains, how versioning works, and how to contribute.

Content to include:
1. Overview: spec/ contains the QD Framework artifact distribution — skills, hooks, scripts, and reference documentation that gets installed into IDE projects via 'qd init'.

2. Artifacts explained:
   - hooks/: Session lifecycle hooks (PreToolUse, SessionStart, Stop, etc.)
   - skills/: 20 skill packages (planning, reviewing, debugging, etc.) each with SKILL.md
   - scripts/: Runtime scripts (qd_dependencies.mjs, qd_state.mjs)
   - module.yaml: Artifact manifest (version, skip rules, format conversions)
   - hooks.json: Hook type to command mappings

3. Versioning: Spec uses bare semver (v0.1.0, v0.2.0) — NOT prefixed with 'spec-'. Push a tag to trigger GitHub Release.

4. Release process: git tag v0.x.x && git push --tags → CI runs tests → creates GitHub Release with qdspec-artifacts.tar.gz

5. Testing locally:
   - npm test (schema + content tests)
   - npm run smoke (structural smoke)
   - npm run smoke:install (E2E — requires qd CLI built)
   - QD_SPEC_PATH=./artifacts npm run smoke:install (test against local artifacts)

6. How qd init uses spec: The qd CLI downloads the spec tarball from GitHub Releases at the selected version and extracts it to a temp dir for installation.

title: Update init.ts — IS_DEV path + QD_SPEC_PATH env var support
description:
Update src/commands/init.ts — update IS_DEV path and add QD_SPEC_PATH env var support.

Changes to make:

1. Find the IS_DEV block (around line 38-42):
   OLD: artifactsDir = path.join(qdProjectRoot, 'artifacts');
   NEW: artifactsDir = process.env.QD_SPEC_PATH
          ?? path.join(qdProjectRoot, '..', 'spec', 'artifacts');

   This makes IS_DEV mode point to sibling spec/ directory instead of local artifacts/.

2. The QD_SPEC_PATH env var is checked FIRST before the path join. This allows:
   - spec developers to point at their local spec: QD_SPEC_PATH=/home/quangdang/projects/spec/artifacts
   - The sibling path is the default: <qd-repo>/../spec/artifacts

3. QD_ENV=development is already checked first (line 16). Keep that check.

4. Verify that in production mode (IS_DEV=false, no QD_SPEC_PATH): 
   the code already uses downloadVersion() from GitHub — no changes needed there.

5. IMPORTANT: In the fallback block (lines 55-57), the fallback to 
   'projectDir/artifacts' should stay as-is — that's offline fallback behavior,
   not the dev path.

After making changes:
- Run: grep -n 'QD_SPEC_PATH\|spec.*artifacts' src/commands/init.ts to verify changes
- Run: grep -n 'artifactsDir.*path.join' src/commands/init.ts to verify the new path

This bead blocks qd-15z (npm publish workflow).

title: Phase 7: First release — tag v0.1.0 and 0.1.0
close_reason: Completed: All code ready. Manual steps remain: git tag -a v0.1.0 + git push, then git tag -a 0.1.0 + git push. Build/tests pass.

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text:
## Phase 7: First Release - Tags v0.1.0 and 0.1.0

### WHY
First release validates the entire workflow end-to-end: artifacts release on GitHub, CLI publish on npm.

### STEPS
1. Verify everything works in dev mode:
   QD_ENV=development npm run build
   node dist/index.js init --ides claude-code

2. Tag artifacts (v prefix) - triggers release-artifacts.yml:
   git tag -a v0.1.0 -m "Release artifacts v0.1.0"
   git push origin v0.1.0
   -> GitHub Release created automatically with qdspec-artifacts.tar.gz + .zip

3. Tag CLI (no prefix) - triggers publish-cli.yml:
   git tag -a 0.1.0 -m "Release CLI v0.1.0"
   git push origin 0.1.0
   -> npm publish triggered to npmjs.org/qdspec

### MANUAL STEPS
1. Create GitHub repo for qd if it does not exist
2. Ensure NPM_TOKEN secret is added to repository
3. Verify both workflows exist in .github/workflows/

### WHAT NOT TO DO
- Do NOT run both tags at the exact same time (CLI workflow may not see artifacts release yet)
- Wait for GitHub Release to appear before pushing CLI tag

### TEST OBLIGATIONS
- GitHub Release appears at github.com/quangdang46/qd/releases with v0.1.0
- Release contains qdspec-artifacts.tar.gz and qdspec-artifacts.zip
- npmjs.org shows qdspec version 0.1.0
- npx qdspec init works from npm (not dev mode)
- Downloaded artifacts match local artifacts/ content

title: Write spec/test/test-schema-validation.ts — Jest tests for module.yaml, hooks.json, SKILL.md frontmatter
description:
Write spec/test/test-schema-validation.ts — Jest test suite enforcing artifact contracts.

Purpose: Enforce that module.yaml, hooks.json, and all SKILL.md frontmatter files conform to their schemas. Runs in CI on every push.

Test categories to implement:

1. SKILL.md frontmatter (for EVERY skill in artifacts/skills/*/SKILL.md):
   - Frontmatter parses as valid YAML
   - name field: non-empty string, kebab-case (^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$)
   - description field: non-empty string
   - metadata object exists
   - metadata.ecosystem: must be "qd" (NOT "qd-framework" or other)
   - metadata.dependencies: if present, each entry has {id, kind, missing_effect, reason}
   - Each dependency.id: non-empty string
   - Each dependency.kind: one of "command", "file", "skill"
   - Each dependency.missing_effect: one of "unavailable", "warn", "error"

2. module.yaml schema:
   - code field: non-empty string
   - name field: non-empty string
   - description field: non-empty string
   - skip: if present, must be array of strings
   - convert: if present, each IDE key maps to object of pattern->format
   - No unknown top-level keys

3. hooks.json schema:
   - hooks object exists
   - Known hook types only: "SessionStart", "PreToolUse", "Stop"
   - Each hook entry has hooks array (non-empty)
   - Each hook entry may have matcher (string)
   - Each hook in hooks[] has: type ("command"), command (string), statusMessage (string)
   - Every command value resolves to a file in artifacts/hooks/
   - No duplicate hook types

4. Cross-reference:
   - Every .mjs file in artifacts/hooks/ is referenced in hooks.json
   - No orphan hook files

Reuse patterns from qd/test/schema-validation.test.ts where applicable. This file is standalone — it cannot import from qd/src/.

title: Phase 6: Create publish-cli.yml — npm publish workflow
close_reason: Completed: publish-cli.yml created with npm publish on semver tag (no v prefix), NODE_AUTH_TOKEN from secrets

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text:
## Phase 6: Create publish-cli.yml - NPM Publish Workflow

### WHY
GitHub Actions must publish the CLI to npm when a semver tag (no v prefix) is pushed.

### WHAT
Create `.github/workflows/publish-cli.yml`:

### TAG FILTER NOTE
GitHub Actions on.push.tags glob does NOT support negative matching.
Pattern [0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+ matches BOTH 0.1.0 and v0.1.0.
Use if: "!startsWith(github.ref_name, v')" guard to skip v-prefixed tags.

### Workflow
```yaml
name: Publish CLI to npm
on:
  push:
    tags:
      - '[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+'

jobs:
  publish:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: "!startsWith(github.ref_name, 'v')"
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          registry-url: 'https://registry.npmjs.org'
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run build
      - run: npm publish
        env:
          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
```

### Prerequisites
- NPM_TOKEN secret must be added to repository settings
- package.json publishConfig must have access: public

### TEST OBLIGATIONS
- Workflow triggers on tag 0.1.0 (not v0.1.0)
- Workflow does NOT trigger on tag v0.1.0
- npm publish is called with correct NODE_AUTH_TOKEN

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text:
## Phase 1: Download + Extract (download.ts)

### WHY
The CLI must download the GitHub release tarball, extract it to a temp dir, and discover the artifacts/ subdir inside. Cache avoids re-downloading the same version.

### WHAT
Create `src/domains/github/download.ts` implementing:

#### Cache
- Store in ~/.cache/qdspec/
- Key: v{tag}-{sha}.tar.gz (sha = git commit hash of the tag)
- TTL: none (only invalidated by --no-cache flag)

#### Download
- Use node:https GET request (no external deps)
- Download to os.tmpdir() + /qd-artifacts-{uuid}/
- Show progress with @clack/prompts spinner

#### Extract
- Unix/macOS/WSL: child_process.exec(tar -xzf ...)
- Windows native: use extract-zip npm package (add to deps)
- Detect platform: process.platform === win32

#### Dynamic Path Discovery
GitHub archive structure: qd-{tag}/artifacts/ (NOT artifacts/ at root)
Must find the qd-{tag}/ subdir dynamically after extract.

#### Cleanup
- Remove temp dir after phase 3 walk complete
- Keep cache indefinitely

### TEST OBLIGATIONS
- Cache hit returns cached path without re-download
- Cache miss downloads and extracts correctly
- --no-cache bypasses cache and re-downloads
- Extract handles qd-{tag}/artifacts/ path correctly
- Temp dir is removed after use
- Cache dir is created if it doesn't exist

title: Phase 4: Update init.ts — version selection + download orchestration
close_reason: Completed: init.ts updated with version selection, download orchestration, dev mode QD_ENV, rate limit fallback, --version and --no-cache flags

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text:
## Phase 4: Update init.ts - Version Selection + Download Orchestration

### WHY
init.ts is the entry point. It must orchestrate version selection and download before passing artifactsDir to Installer.install().

### WHAT
Modify `src/commands/init.ts`:

#### New flow
1. Parse options (--ides, --directory, --version, --no-cache)
2. DEV MODE check: if QD_ENV === development or NODE_ENV === development, use local ./artifacts/
3. Determine artifactsDir:
   - if --version flag -> download specific tag directly
   - else -> interactive version selector -> download selected
   - Rate limited or offline -> fallback to local artifacts/ (if exists)
4. Pass artifactsDir to Installer.install()
5. Cleanup temp dir (keep cache)

#### New options to add
.option(--version <vX.Y.Z>, Install specific artifacts version from GitHub)
.option(--no-cache, Bypass artifact cache, re-download even if cached)

#### DEV MODE
```javascript
if (isDevMode()) {
  const localPath = path.join(projectDir, 'artifacts');
  if (fs.pathExists(localPath)) return localPath;
  throw new Error('DEV mode requires local artifacts/ directory');
}
```

#### Fallback logic
If GitHub API fails (rate limit, network) and local artifacts/ exists, show message and use local.

### ERROR HANDLING
- Rate limit: clear message + suggestion to set GITHUB_TOKEN + fallback to local
- Invalid tag: Error Tag not found + show available versions
- No local artifacts + offline: Error with actionable message

### REFERENCES
See current src/commands/init.ts for existing structure

### TEST OBLIGATIONS
- QD_ENV=development uses local artifacts/
- qd init (no args) fetches from GitHub and shows version picker
- qd init --version v0.1.0 downloads specific tag
- qd init --version v0.1.0 --no-cache bypasses cache
- Rate limit shows error + fallback message
- Offline + no local = actionable error
